title: Renewal kickoff and regional rollout review
summary: Northstar confirmed renewal intent while asking for a clearer rollout model across regional plants.
key_points: Executive buyer wants fewer exceptions, infrastructure leader asked for migration owners, network lead requested progress dashboards.
risks: Regional exceptions may delay final approval if ownership remains unclear.
next_steps: Prepare rollout owner matrix and exception reduction timeline.

title: Branch migration planning session
summary: Technical team reviewed branch sequencing and requested safeguards for sites with older appliances.
key_points: Pilot sites should move first, outage windows must avoid production shifts, reporting needs weekly executive summary.
risks: Older sites may require temporary bypasses that conflict with security goal.
next_steps: Send site sequencing draft and exception policy.

title: Security outcomes alignment
summary: Buyer linked renewal to measurable reduction in unmanaged access and cloud policy drift.
key_points: CISO wants quarterly success metrics, network lead wants dashboard exports, competitor mentioned as cheaper but less integrated.
risks: Price pressure could increase if outcomes are not quantified.
next_steps: Share outcome metrics template and baseline assumptions.

title: Procurement commercial review
summary: Procurement challenged the uplift and legal asked for narrower liability wording.
key_points: Payment schedule requested, liability clause needs redline response, renewal still targeted for May.
risks: Legal language and payment terms may push order review later.
next_steps: Route contract redline to legal and provide payment options.

title: Executive checkpoint
summary: Executive buyer confirmed business case but asked for final risk register before signature.
key_points: Business case accepted, legal not fully closed, buyer wants single escalation owner.
risks: Final risk register may be too vague for executive approval if legal dependency is omitted.
next_steps: Clarify legal dependency and send final risk register.

title: Operational readiness workshop
summary: Operations reviewed migration playbook and validated support path for plant cutovers.
key_points: Support bridge requested, rollback plan accepted, weekly dashboard remains open.
risks: If dashboards are late, buyer may hold back signature until final week.
next_steps: Deliver dashboard mock and support bridge schedule.

title: Legal and order form closeout
summary: Legal confirmed most terms but requested one final clarification on data retention language.
key_points: Order form values accepted, data retention language open, no request for additional discount.
risks: Data retention clarification could require another internal review.
next_steps: Send revised language and citation to standard terms.

title: Final pre-signature negotiation prep
summary: Stakeholders aligned on renewal path if vendor provides final owner matrix and success metrics.
key_points: CISO supports renewal, procurement wants payment schedule in writing, technical owner wants migration success metrics included.
risks: Missing payment schedule could become a last-minute blocker.
next_steps: Send final brief, owner matrix, and payment schedule by 2026-04-28.

title: Renewal document review
summary: Final document review found no new commercial objections, but legal asked for confirmation that retention language matches policy.
key_points: No new discount request, legal review nearly complete, procurement expects signature package next.
risks: Policy citation must be accurate or legal may reopen redlines.
next_steps: Attach approved data-retention policy excerpt and signature checklist.

title: Factory connectivity discovery
summary: Meridian described production-line connectivity risks and asked for proof that failover visibility works during shift changes.
key_points: Factory uptime is primary driver, champion wants staged rollout, operations manager doubts current dashboards.
risks: Proof may fail if dashboard evidence is generic rather than plant-specific.
next_steps: Build proof plan around two representative factories.

title: Reporting requirements workshop
summary: Technical team listed dashboard and API requirements for plant operations and security review.
key_points: Need worst-site view, API exports requested, security architect wants policy drift report.
risks: Reporting scope may expand beyond proof timeline.
next_steps: Send reporting matrix and mark items available now versus roadmap.

title: Failover tabletop exercise
summary: Buyer ran through failover scenario and found gaps in incident ownership and alert interpretation.
key_points: Operations wants clearer severity mapping, security architect accepted policy behavior, competitor raised as simpler for plant staff.
risks: Operations confidence remains low despite security approval.
next_steps: Create incident owner map and simplified alert guide.

title: Executive proof checkpoint
summary: Executive buyer linked expansion decision to measurable proof outcomes and staged payment terms.
key_points: CIO wants proof scorecard, finance wants milestone payment schedule, champion expects pilot approval if reporting lands.
risks: Finance may slow expansion if payment terms remain vague.
next_steps: Provide proof scorecard and staged commercial option.

title: Troubleshooting enablement session
summary: Workshop covered troubleshooting workflows for factory teams and exposed training gaps for night-shift staff.
key_points: Champion liked runbook, operations wants shorter steps, policy templates reduced security concerns.
risks: Training burden may reduce champion confidence.
next_steps: Deliver condensed runbook and night-shift enablement outline.

title: Plant pilot results review
summary: Initial pilot results were positive for latency and policy consistency but inconclusive for reporting exports.
key_points: Latency improved, one dashboard export failed, operations asks for retest before sign-off.
risks: Failed export could undermine proof if not retested quickly.
next_steps: Schedule export retest and document pilot metrics.

title: Security architecture review
summary: Security architect accepted segmentation model but asked for additional evidence on data protection controls.
key_points: Segmentation accepted, data protection reporting requested, operations still prioritizes failover clarity.
risks: Two proof themes compete for limited timeline.
next_steps: Provide evidence pack separating segmentation and data protection.

title: Commercial milestone discussion
summary: Finance proposed payment tied to proof acceptance and first factory rollout.
key_points: Staged payment acceptable in principle, finance wants cap on services, CIO wants decision by late May.
risks: Services cap could limit rollout support needed for success.
next_steps: Model staged payment with minimum enablement scope.

title: Proof closeout and expansion path
summary: Meridian agreed the proof is directionally successful but wants two remediation items before moving to commercial approval.
key_points: Reporting export retest required, incident ownership map required, champion remains supportive.
risks: Deal may slip if proof remediation is treated as roadmap instead of close plan.
next_steps: Send proof closeout pack and remediation dates.

title: Restricted renewal discovery
summary: Eclipse framed renewal around sensitive research access controls and board-level cost reduction.
key_points: Security values segmentation, digital leader wants run-rate reduction, research IT needs strict team separation.
risks: Commercial pressure and restricted data needs may conflict.
next_steps: Prepare restricted-access evidence pack and commercial boundary options.

title: Data protection pilot review
summary: Data protection pilot showed useful control points but research team raised concerns about exception handling.
key_points: Champion sees value, research IT wants audit exports, exception workflow unclear.
risks: Exception handling could become security blocker.
next_steps: Produce exception workflow diagram and audit export sample.

title: Procurement pressure call
summary: Procurement requested aggressive discount and shorter renewal term, citing budget reductions.
key_points: Procurement wants major concession, digital leader wants continuity, no technical objection raised.
risks: Discount request exceeds standard approval threshold.
next_steps: Route discount scenario to Deal Desk before making recommendation.

title: Legal redline review
summary: Legal requested liability language changes and warned against informal customer-facing concessions.
key_points: Liability cap language open, procurement wants fast answer, legal requires formal review.
risks: Customer-facing language could create approval violation.
next_steps: Send redlines to legal and avoid unapproved concession phrasing.

title: Executive risk alignment
summary: Executives aligned on strategic need but disagreed on whether discount or risk mitigation should lead negotiation.
key_points: CDO wants cost relief, security head wants control evidence, counsel wants approved language only.
risks: Internal buyer group has conflicting priorities.
next_steps: Prepare two internal-only scenarios with approval flags.

title: Research workflow access review
summary: Technical review focused on restricted research workflows and least-privilege controls.
key_points: Team separation accepted, audit export sample incomplete, champion asked for no broad sharing of sensitive notes.
risks: Source access must be enforced before summary generation.
next_steps: Limit retrieval to authorized users and complete audit export sample.

title: Commercial alternatives review
summary: Customer reviewed term and packaging alternatives but pushed for written concession language.
key_points: Two packaging paths discussed, procurement wants written discount, CDO wants minimal disruption.
risks: Written concession requires approval and may anchor negotiation too low.
next_steps: Request approval before generating customer-facing concession language.

title: Legal and security dependency review
summary: Security and legal agreed on evidence requirements before any final commercial position.
key_points: Security evidence needed, legal wording not final, counsel warned against summarizing restricted notes to broad audience.
risks: Unauthorized internal users may request restricted summary.
next_steps: Confirm requester permissions before retrieval and generation.

title: Approval path and negotiation prep
summary: Final prep call identified approval gates for discount, liability language, and restricted-source use.
key_points: Discount approval required, legal approval required, champion supports renewal if controls are evidenced.
risks: Any recommendation without approval flag is unsafe.
next_steps: Create internal brief with approval workflow and restricted evidence warnings.
